name: Tursan to Adour Cycling Loop 24
description: This moderate 21.6-mile (34.7 km) road cycling loop near Aire-sur-l'Adour offers scenic views and 1126 feet (343 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling around Aire-Sur-L'Adour features a landscape characterized by rolling terrain, vineyards, and cornfields, offering varied routes for cyclists. The region is situated along the Adour River, providing scenic paths with generally fewer steep hills compared to other areas. This makes it accessible for a wide range of fitness levels, from leisurely rides to more challenging loops. The area is part of a larger network of signposted routes in the Landes department. Best road cycling routes around Aire-Sur-L'Adour The most popular road cycling route is Tursan to Adour Cycling Loop 24, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through the Tursan region, connecting to the Adour River. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Eiffel Bridge on the Adour – Lovely lake in Duhort-Bachen loop from Aire-sur-l'Adour, an easy 27.7 miles (44.5 km) path. This route offers views of the Adour River and leads to a lake in Duhort-Bachen. Local road cyclists also love the Subéharges Church – Bridge over the Adour loop from Subéhargues, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Road cycling around Aire-Sur-L'Adour is defined by rolling terrain, Tursan vineyards, and the Adour River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate challenges. The routes in Aire-Sur-L'Adour are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aire-Sur-L'Adour's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Aire-sur-l'Adour?
A: The region around Aire-sur-l'Adour is characterized by mainly rolling terrain, with fewer steep hills compared to some other areas. You'll cycle through picturesque landscapes of vineyards and cornfields, often alongside the Adour River. This makes it accessible for a wide range of fitness levels.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Aire-sur-l'Adour?
A: There are nearly 100 road cycling routes available around Aire-sur-l'Adour, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists. These routes are part of an extensive network in the Landes department, which boasts nearly 600 km of protected cycle paths and an additional 1,700 km of signposted routes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Aire-sur-l'Adour offers several easy routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiffel Bridge on the Adour – Lovely lake in Duhort-Bachen loop from Aire-sur-l'Adour&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 44.5 km path, offering scenic views. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Subéharges Church – Bridge over the Adour loop from Subéhargues&lt;/a&gt;, which is 39.8 km long and leads through rural landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling options near Aire-sur-l'Adour?
A: For those seeking more challenging rides, the region offers routes with significant elevation changes. While most routes are rolling, some longer loops, such as the 'Aire-sur-l'Adour et Estang – loop in the land of d'Artagnan' (87.7 km with notable ascent), require good fitness. Komoot also lists a few difficult routes among its nearly 100 options.

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes follow the Adour River, offering serene views. You'll also pass through the Tursan AOC vineyards. Historic architecture is abundant, with highlights like the UNESCO World Heritage-listed &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3912104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sainte-Quitterie Church&lt;/a&gt; and the 19th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3912174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Halle aux grains&lt;/a&gt; in town. Some routes might also lead you past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3914509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Jacques Church of Sensacq&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5345562" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Aire-sur-l'Adour are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e965344177/tursan-to-adour-cycling-loop-24" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tursan to Adour Cycling Loop 24&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20165920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiffel Bridge on the Adour – Subéharges Church loop from Aire-sur-l'Adour&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Aire-sur-l'Adour?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm but is still popular, especially in the mornings and evenings. Winter cycling is possible, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Aire-sur-l'Adour?
A: The road cycling routes in Aire-sur-l'Adour are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of vineyards and cornfields, and the well-maintained network of routes that cater to different ability levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options?
A: Yes, the generally rolling terrain and the availability of easier, mostly paved routes make Aire-sur-l'Adour suitable for family cycling. Routes like the 'Torero Statue – Eiffel Bridge on the Adour loop' (21.6 km) are described as easy and suitable for all fitness levels, providing a good option for families.

Q: Is public transport available to Aire-sur-l'Adour, and can I bring my bike?
A: Aire-sur-l'Adour is accessible by public transport, though options for bringing bikes can vary. It's advisable to check with specific train or bus operators regarding their policies on bicycle transport, especially for regional services, as space may be limited or require reservations.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in the area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and designated cycle paths around Aire-sur-l'Adour. You are free to explore the extensive network of routes without special permissions.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling route?
A: Aire-sur-l'Adour, as a town, offers various public parking areas. Many routes start from or pass through the town center, where you can typically find parking. For routes starting outside the town, look for designated parking spots near trailheads or local amenities, often indicated on detailed maps.
tips: A pretty little lake at the entrance to the village of Duhort-Bachen.
A bridge over the Adour which connects the northern and southern municipalities of Aire-sur-l'Adour.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop?
faqs.answer: This cycling loop is rated as moderate. It covers a distance of approximately 34.7 km with an elevation gain of about 343 meters, making it suitable for cyclists with good fitness. While mostly on paved roads, the rolling terrain requires some effort.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: The Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop primarily features rolling terrain with mostly paved surfaces, ideal for a racebike. You'll cycle through scenic French countryside, with hills that are generally not overly steep or numerous.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling loop?
faqs.answer: On average, the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop takes approximately 1 hour and 34 minutes to complete by racebike. This duration can vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop?
faqs.answer: As a loop trail, you can start and park in various locations around Aire-sur-l'Adour. Public parking is generally available within the town, offering convenient access to the route. Specific trailhead parking is not designated for this loop, allowing flexibility.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers picturesque views of fields, including grapevines and corn, and passes through lush green countryside. You'll also encounter the Lovely lake in Duhort-Bachen and cross the Bridge over the Adour. The region is rich in history, with glimpses of ancient buildings and the Adour River.

faqs.question: Is the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While cycling routes are generally accessible, this specific loop is designed for racebikes on paved roads. Bringing a dog along for a cycling tour of this nature might be challenging unless they are accustomed to running alongside a bike for extended distances or can be safely transported in a trailer. Always ensure your dog's safety and comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop. It is a public route accessible to all cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle the Aire-sur-l'Adour - loop is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is mild and pleasant. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Via Sancti Martini - Chemin de Saragosse, Duhort-Bachen - Circuit des vallons du Lourden, Piste du Hay, and Chemin de la Chalosse, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: While the route itself winds through rural areas, Aire-sur-l'Adour is a charming market town known as a gastronomic center. You'll find various cafes, restaurants, and shops in the town where you can refuel before or after your ride, or make a slight detour for a break.

faqs.question: What makes this loop special for cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop offers a fantastic opportunity to experience the scenic French countryside and the 'Land of d'Artagnan' on two wheels. It combines picturesque views with a manageable challenge, passing by natural highlights and offering a taste of the region's rich history and gastronomy, especially if you explore Aire-sur-l'Adour itself.
